name: Negroni
description: A classic spirit-forward aperitif with equal parts gin substitute—here, mezcal's smoky complexity replaces gin—balanced against Campari's bitter-sweet profile and sweet vermouth's richness. Stirred and served up with an orange twist.
steps: Add mezcal, Campari, and sweet vermouth to a mixing glass with ice
Stir for 10–15 seconds until well-chilled
Strain into a coupe or rocks glass over fresh ice
Express orange twist over the drink and drop in as garnish
why: Mezcal's earthy smoke plays beautifully against Campari's herbal bitterness, creating a sophisticated, spirit-forward drink that anchors a dinner party. The vermouth adds depth without sweetness dominating.

name: Manhattan (Rye)
description: A timeless spirit-forward classic built on rye whiskey's spicy backbone, softened by sweet vermouth and anchored with aromatic bitters. Stirred cold and served up with a cherry.
steps: Add rye, sweet vermouth, and bitters to a mixing glass with ice
Stir for 10–15 seconds until well-chilled and diluted
Strain into a coupe or Nick & Nora glass
Garnish with a cherry (if available) or lemon twist
why: Rittenhouse rye's high rye content delivers spice and structure that shines in this canonical cocktail. The Carpano's vanilla and herbal notes complement the whiskey's character, making it an elegant, warming choice for dinner guests.

name: Negroni
description: A classic aperitivo cocktail with equal parts gin substitute, Campari, and sweet vermouth, stirred and served up or over ice. This version uses mezcal for a smoky, bracing edge.
steps: Add mezcal, Campari, sweet vermouth, and bitters to a mixing glass filled with ice
Stir for 10–15 seconds until well-chilled
Strain into a coupe or serve over a large ice cube in a rocks glass
Express an orange twist over the drink if available
why: The Negroni is the quintessential bitter, bracing aperitivo. Mezcal replaces gin here, adding smoke and complexity while keeping the drink dry and spirit-forward. Campari's herbal bitterness and the vermouth's depth make this perfectly suited to aperitivo hour without any cloying sweetness.

name: Hot Toddy
description: A warming, soothing nightcap of bourbon, honey, lemon, and hot water—a timeless comfort drink perfect for winding down.
steps: Warm a mug with hot water, then discard
Add honey and lemon juice to the mug
Pour in 2 oz Four Roses Small Batch
Top with 4 oz hot water and stir well
Optional: garnish with a lemon wheel or cinnamon stick
why: A Hot Toddy is the quintessential warming nightcap—it's simple, requires only what you have on hand, and the bourbon's vanilla and caramel notes pair beautifully with honey and citrus. Perfect for relaxing before bed.

name: Tropical Bourbon Punch
description: A refreshing, fruity tiki-style punch built on bourbon with bright citrus and tropical fruit juices, perfect for a beach party atmosphere.
steps: Fill a tiki mug or tall glass with crushed ice
Pour the Four Roses Small Batch over the ice
Add pineapple juice, orange juice, and fresh lime juice
Top with simple syrup and stir well
Garnish with a pineapple wedge and cherry if available
why: This drink maximizes your bourbon while delivering the tropical, fruity vibe you're after. The citrus and pineapple juice complement Four Roses' vanilla and spice notes beautifully, and it's easy to batch for a beach party crowd.

name: Old Fashioned
description: A timeless bourbon cocktail with deep caramel and subtle spice notes, built on the foundation of quality whiskey.
steps: Place sugar and water in a rocks glass
Stir until sugar dissolves
Add a large ice cube or several smaller cubes
Pour in 2 oz Four Roses Small Batch
Stir gently for 10 seconds
why: This classic requires only what you have on hand. Four Roses Small Batch's balanced vanilla and spice profile shines in this simple format.

name: Bourbon Neat
description: A pure, unadulterated pour that lets the whiskey speak for itself—perfect for savoring the craftsmanship.
steps: Pour 2 oz Four Roses Small Batch into a rocks glass
Add ice if desired, or drink at room temperature
Sip slowly and observe the flavors evolving
why: Sometimes the best drink is the simplest. Four Roses Small Batch is excellent enough to enjoy on its own, revealing notes of caramel, oak, and floral complexity.

name: Bourbon & Water
description: A gentle, hydrating serve that opens up the whiskey's subtle flavors while keeping you refreshed.
steps: Fill a rocks glass with ice
Pour in 1.5 oz Four Roses Small Batch
Top with 1.5 oz water
Stir briefly and enjoy
why: Adding water to bourbon is a time-honored practice that releases aromatic compounds and makes the whiskey more approachable for a casual evening.

name: Smoky Negroni
description: A spirit-forward twist on the classic Negroni, swapping traditional gin for mezcal to create a deeply smoky, complex aperitif with bitter and herbal notes. Stirred until silky and served up or on the rocks.
steps: Add mezcal, Campari, dry vermouth, and Angostura bitters to a mixing glass
Fill with ice and stir for 30 seconds until well-chilled
Strain into a coupe glass or over fresh ice in a rocks glass
Express an orange twist over the drink and drop it in as garnish
why: This drink plays directly to your wife's preferences—mezcal-forward, spirit-driven, and bone-dry with no added sweetness. The smoke from the mezcal pairs beautifully with Campari's bitter complexity and the herbal backbone of dry vermouth. It's an elegant, intimate aperitif perfect for a date night.

name: Mezcal Paloma
description: A bright, citrusy tequila-style cocktail built on smoky mezcal with fresh lime and grapefruit, perfect for taco night.
steps: Add mezcal, Cointreau, lime juice, grapefruit juice, and simple syrup to a cocktail shaker with ice
Shake vigorously for 10–12 seconds
Strain into a salt-rimmed glass filled with fresh ice
Garnish with a lime wheel
why: Mezcal's smoky character pairs beautifully with the savory, spiced flavors of tacos, while the bright citrus and Cointreau's orange notes keep it refreshing and festive. This hits your bright and citrusy vibe perfectly.

name: Sazerac
description: A classic New Orleans cocktail with rye whiskey, absinthe rinse, and aromatic bitters—bold, warming, and perfect for settling in during a cozy movie night.
steps: Rinse a rocks glass with absinthe (or skip if unavailable) and discard excess
Muddle sugar and water in a mixing glass
Add rye and bitters, stir with ice until chilled
Strain into the prepared glass over fresh ice
Express lemon peel oils over the drink and drop in as garnish
why: The Sazerac is quintessentially cozy—warming rye, aromatic bitters, and a citrus finish make it ideal for movie night. You have everything needed without substitutions.

name: Negroni
description: A classic Italian aperitivo cocktail with equal parts gin, Campari, and sweet vermouth, stirred with ice and garnished with an orange twist. Bittersweet, spirit-forward, and perfectly balanced.
steps: Add 1 oz gin, 1 oz Campari, and 1 oz Carpano Antica to a mixing glass filled with ice
Stir for 10–15 seconds until well-chilled
Strain into a rocks glass over fresh ice
Express an orange twist over the drink and drop it in as garnish
why: You have two of the three essential Negroni ingredients—Campari and sweet vermouth—both perfect for this bitter, classic aperitivo. You'll need to acquire gin to complete the drink, but once you do, this is the quintessential pre-dinner cocktail that embodies the mood you're after.

name: Sazerac
description: A New Orleans classic rye whiskey cocktail with a rinse of absinthe, balanced with Peychaud's bitters and a touch of sugar. Traditionally served in a chilled glass with a lemon twist.
steps: Chill a rocks glass with ice and water, then discard
Rinse the glass with absinthe, coating the interior
In a mixing glass, combine rye, Peychaud's bitters, sugar, and water
Stir with ice until well-chilled
Strain into the absinthe-rinsed glass
Express lemon peel oils over the drink and drop as garnish
why: You have the foundational spirit (Rittenhouse Rye), but the Sazerac requires two specialty bottles—Peychaud's Bitters and absinthe—that aren't in your current collection. Angostura Bitters won't substitute authentically. This is the drink you've requested, but you'll need to acquire those two missing ingredients to make it properly.

name: Mai Tai
description: A classic tropical rum cocktail with a perfect balance of sweet, tart, and complex rum flavors, finished with a float of dark rum for depth.
steps: Add light rum, dark rum, Curaçao, orgeat syrup, fresh lime juice, and simple syrup to a cocktail shaker with ice
Shake vigorously for 10-12 seconds
Strain into a rocks glass filled with crushed ice
Float 0.5 oz of dark rum on top by pouring slowly over the back of a bar spoon
Garnish with a lime wheel and mint sprig
why: The Mai Tai is the quintessential tropical beach party cocktail—it's vibrant, refreshing, and instantly evokes island vibes. However, you'll need to acquire light rum, dark rum, Curaçao, and orgeat syrup, as your current inventory only contains bourbon.

name: Old Fashioned
description: A timeless bourbon cocktail with deep caramel and spice notes, stirred down and served over ice with a citrus twist.
steps: Muddle sugar and water in a rocks glass
Add bourbon and stir
Fill glass with ice
Express orange peel oils over the drink and drop in as garnish
why: A perfect showcase for Four Roses' smooth, floral bourbon character. Requires only pantry staples and a citrus peel you likely have.

name: Sazerac-Style Rye
description: A spicy, herbaceous rye cocktail with absinthe rinse and Peychaud's bitters—a New Orleans classic adapted to what you have.
steps: Rinse a rocks glass with absinthe and discard excess
Add rye, simple syrup, and bitters to a mixing glass with ice
Stir and strain into the prepared glass
Express lemon peel and drop in
why: Rittenhouse rye's high rye content shines in this bold, spirit-forward drink. You'll need specialty bitters and absinthe to complete it properly.

name: Mezcal Margarita
description: A smoky, complex take on the classic with Del Maguey's earthy mezcal, lime, and a touch of sweetness.
steps: Rim a coupe or rocks glass with salt
Add mezcal, lime juice, and simple syrup to a shaker with ice
Shake vigorously and strain into the prepared glass
Serve up or over ice
why: Del Maguey Vida's bright, vegetal profile makes an excellent mezcal margarita. Fresh lime is essential; this drink lives or dies by citrus quality.

name: Boulevardier
description: A rye-based cousin of the Negroni with herbal depth, stirred and served up—sophisticated and warming.
steps: Add rye, sweet vermouth, and Campari to a mixing glass with ice
Stir for 30 seconds
Strain into a coupe glass
Express orange twist over the drink and drop in
why: Rittenhouse's spicy backbone pairs beautifully with vermouth and Campari. You'll need to acquire both vermouth and Campari for this one.

name: Mezcal Negroni
description: A smoky, bitter-forward stirred cocktail that swaps gin for mezcal, creating an earthy, complex sipper.
steps: Add mezcal, Campari, and sweet vermouth to a mixing glass with ice
Stir for 30 seconds
Strain into a coupe or rocks glass
Express orange twist and drop in as garnish
why: Del Maguey's smoke and minerality create a distinctive, contemplative Negroni. Campari and sweet vermouth are the missing pieces.

name: Mezcal Negroni
description: A smoky, spirit-forward aperitif with bitter and herbal notes—the mezcal replaces gin for a distinctly earthy character.
steps: Stir mezcal, Campari, and Carpano Antica with ice until well-chilled
Strain into a rocks glass over fresh ice
Express orange peel oils over the drink and drop as garnish
why: Plays directly to Alex's love of smoky mezcal while delivering the complex, bitter-herbal profile they prefer. The Campari and vermouth add depth without cloying sweetness.

name: Cointreau Sidecar
description: A classic brandy cocktail with bright citrus and subtle sweetness—smooth, approachable, and fruit-forward.
steps: Shake Four Roses, Cointreau, and fresh lemon juice with ice
Strain into a coupe or cocktail glass
Serve up
why: Sam gravitates toward sweet and fruity drinks, and this delivers both: the Cointreau's orange liqueur sweetness pairs beautifully with bright lemon, while the bourbon adds warmth without bitterness.

name: Negroni
description: A classic equal-parts aperitivo cocktail balancing bitter Campari, herbal vermouth, and warming bourbon—ideal for aperitivo hour with bold, dry flavors.
steps: Add bourbon, Campari, and sweet vermouth to a mixing glass with ice
Stir for 10–15 seconds until well-chilled
Strain into a rocks glass over fresh ice
Add 2 dashes of Angostura bitters
Express an orange twist over the drink and drop it in as garnish
why: Jamie loves bitter and amaro flavors and dislikes sweet—this drink leads with Campari's dry bitterness and herbal complexity, with just enough vermouth sweetness to round it out. It's the quintessential aperitivo, designed to open the palate.
